title: Separate AW advisory signals from agent-owned decisions

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the first #1243 slice: make AW reportable outputs distinguish hard gates, observed facts, advisory support, candidate routes, and agent-owned decisions.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Preserve existing machine fields where possible; do not remove useful routing, proof, Planning, or delegation guidance; keep real hard gates authoritative.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Presentation-layer field additions, docs wording, focused tests, and generated reference refreshes needed to make the authority boundary legible.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires broad internal field renames, compatibility-breaking schema changes beyond next_safe_action, or solving the broader #1241 human decision-traceability model.
canonical_core.next_action: Finish the authority-boundary runtime/docs/tests slice and run selected proof.
proof_expectations: focused startup, implement, and closeout report tests
completion_criteria: Startup next_safe_action exposes authority_boundary with hard gates, observed facts, recommendations, and agent-owned decisions.
Implement Planning safety/delegation guidance exposes authority_boundary without treating advisory routing as semantic authority.
Closeout report and rendered final-response summary surface the authority boundary for material closeouts.
Docs and generated schema reference explain authority boundaries.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Separate AW advisory signals from agent-owned decisions.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Make AW reportable outputs distinguish hard gates, observed facts, advisory support, candidate routes, and agent-owned decisions.
intent.constraints: Preserve compatibility and useful guidance; keep real hard gates authoritative.
intent.latitude: Add presentation/schema support, docs, tests, and generated references for the authority boundary.
intent.escalation: Escalate before broad internal renames, breaking schema changes, or #1241 human decision-traceability work.
intent.proof: Focused authority-boundary tests passed; make test-workspace passed; make lint-workspace passed; contract tooling passed; structured inventory passed; targeted ruff passed; generated command package check passed; maintainer-surfaces passed; schema-reference-docs passed; planning surfaces passed; AW summary and planning doctor passed.

execution.next_step: Finish implementation and run selected proof.
execution.proof: Focused tests plus make test-workspace, make lint-workspace, contract checks, structured inventory, and schema-reference docs.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Complete #1243 by making AW startup, implement, delegation, Planning safety, and closeout/report UX clearly separate hard gates, observed facts, advisory support, candidate routes, and agent-owned decisions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: Review identifies remaining ambiguous public/report fields after this PR

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Agents and reviewers can inspect authority_boundary instead of overreading decision/classification/route_candidate fields.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Broad internal field renames and the larger #1241 human decision-traceability model.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The new boundary itself exposed AW hard-gate versus advisory wording in dogfooding outputs.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: yes; planning closeout recorded explicit proof input.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement refined #1243 if it aligns with the discussed direction.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make AW outputs legible about what AW enforces, observes, recommends, and leaves to agent judgment.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add authority_boundary to next_safe_action, workflow sufficiency, Planning safety/work-shape guidance, delegation, and closeout report/rendering; update docs and tests.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: src/agentic_workspace/workspace_runtime_primitives.py; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/startup_context.schema.json; .agentic-workspace/docs/reporting-contract.md; docs/reference/startup-context.md; tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py; tests/test_workspace_implement_cli.py; tests/test_workspace_report_cli.py; generated workspace command/schema outputs if regeneration requires them; this execplan and planning state.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 12, including generated reference and generated command surfaces if needed.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused tests for startup/implement/closeout authority boundaries; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; contract checks; structured inventory; schema-reference docs.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broad internal renames, removing guidance fields, or changing semantics of hard gates.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, #1241 decision-traceability implementation, package Planning/Memory internals unless a generated refresh requires them.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from authority_boundary runtime fields, startup schema update, report contract docs, and focused tests.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.
